title: Mechanism, system and method for reducing internal air temperature gradient in a centrally-obscured reflective telescope
abstract: A catadioptric optical system (CDOS) having a centrally obscured reflective telescope, which includes: a telescope compartment defining a telescope space therein, a primary reflector having a central opening and a secondary reflector. The reflectors are located in the telescope compartment. The CDOS also includes a mechanism for reducing temperature gradient in the telescope space. The mechanism includes an air duct having a first opening and a second opening; a hollow enclosure having side openings and one or more airflow generation devices. The mechanism is configured for forming an air passageway between the airflow generation device and the inner telescope space via the air duct and hollow enclosure located therebetween, for reducing internal air temperature gradient in the telescope space.
claims:
Claims 1. A catadioptric optical system comprising: a centrally-obscured reflective telescope, including at least a primary reflector and a secondary reflector and a telescope compartment defining a telescope space therein, said primary and secondary reflectors being located in said telescope compartment, wherein said primary reflector has a central opening; rear refractive optics comprising at least one refractive optical element, said rear refractive optics being located externally from said telescope compartment; a hollow enclosure located between said primary reflector and the nearest refractive optical element of said rear refractive optics, said hollow enclosure having side openings; at least one air duct having a first opening and a second opening; and at least one airflow generation device, wherein the first opening of said air duct faces said at least one airflow generation device, with at least part of the air flow, generated thereby, being directed into said air duct, and the second opening of said air duct faces one side opening of the hollow enclosure, said hollow enclosure other side opening facing said central opening of the primary reflector, for directing the air flow from said at least one airflow generation device to said telescope space via said air duct, for reducing internal air temperature gradient in said telescope space. 2. The catadioptric optical system according to claim 1, wherein said hollow enclosure is located such as to form a gap between said hollow enclosure and the rear side of said primary reflector, said gap being located and configured such as to direct part of the airflow exiting the hollow enclosure over the back side of said primary reflector. 3. The catadioptric optical system according to any one of claims 1 to 2 further comprising a baffle located inside said telescope compartment in the area of the central opening of the primary reflector. 4. The catadioptric optical system according to claim 3, wherein said baffle is a coned baffle being coaxially located in respect to said primary reflector. 5. The catadioptric optical system according to any one of claims 3 to 4, wherein said baffle has at least one perforation located in the vicinity of an optical surface of the primary reflector. 6. The catadioptric optical system according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein said hollow enclosure comprises a spectral beam-splitter made in a form of a hollow pentaprism, and said rear refractive optics farther comprises at least two separate spectral channels. 7. The catadioptric optical system according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein said catadioptric optical system is mounted inside an electro-optical payload, encased by a housing having a front optical window facing said secondary reflector for introducing light therethrough. 8. The catadioptric optical system according to claim 7, wherein said electro-optical payload comprises at least one heat exchanger. 9. The catadioptric optical system according to claim 8, wherein said heat exchanger comprises a plurality of heat conductive elements. 10. The catadioptric optical system according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein said at least one air duct comprises a manifold passageway for allowing directing air flow from said at least one airflow generation device to said telescope compartment and also to other areas in said catadioptric optical system. 11. The catadioptric optical system according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein nitrogen or other gas is used in said catadioptric optical system instead of air. 12. A mechanism for reducing internal air temperature gradients in a catadioptric optical system comprising a centrally-obscured reflective telescope having a telescope compartment defining a telescope space therein, and at least one rear refractive optical element, said mechanism comprising: at least one air duct having a first opening and a second opening; a hollow enclosure having side openings, said hollow enclosure being located at a rear side of said reflective telescope such that one side opening thereof faces a central opening of a primary reflector of the reflective telescope, and another side opening thereof faces the second opening of said air duct; and at least one airflow generation device, wherein the first opening of said air duct faces said at least one airflow generation device, with at least part of the airflow therefrom being directed into said air duct, and the second opening of said air duct is located such as to direct air flow therefrom into the reflective telescope through said side openings in the hollow enclosure, for reducing internal air temperature gradient in said reflective telescope. 13. The mechanism according to claim 12, wherein said hollow enclosure is located such as to form a gap between said hollow enclosure and the rear side of said primary reflector, said gap being located and configured such as to direct part of the airflow exiting the hollow enclosure over the back side of said primary reflector. 14. The mechanism according to any one of claims 12 to 13 further comprising a baffle located inside said telescope compartment next to the opening in the primary reflector. 15. The mechanism according to claim 14, wherein said baffle is a coned baffle being coaxially located in respect to said primary reflector. 16. The mechanism according to any one of claims 14 to 15, wherein said baffle has at least one perforation located next to an optical surface of the primary reflector. 17. The mechanism according to any one of claims 12 to 16, wherein said hollow enclosure comprises a spectral beam-splitter made in a form of a hollow pentaprism, and said rear refractive optical element comprises at least two separate spectral channels. 18. The mechanism according to any one of claims 12 to 17 further comprising at least one heat exchanger. 19. The mechanism according to claim 18, wherein said heat exchanger comprises a plurality of heat conductive elements. 20. The mechanism according to any one of claims 12 to 20, wherein said at least one air duct comprises a manifold passageway for allowing directing air flow from said at least one airflow generation device to multiple areas in said reflective telescope. 21. A method for reducing internal air temperature gradients in a catadioptric optical system with a front centrally-obscured reflective telescope, said method comprising: providing at least one air duct having a first opening and a second opening; 

 providing a hollow enclosure having side openings, said hollow enclosure being located at a rear side of said reflective telescope such that one of its side openings faces a central opening of a primary reflector of said reflective telescope; providing at least one airflow generation device; and ventilating said front centrally-obscured reflective telescope by directing air from said at least one airflow generation device into said front centrally-obscured reflective telescope through said air duct and the side openings of said hollow enclosure, wherein said ventilating of the front centrally-obscured reflective telescope reduces air temperature gradient therein.
